stem: Find the absolute maximum value of \( f(x)=-[[a]](x-([[h]]))^{2}+([[k]]) \).
solution: The downward parabola peaks at its vertex, so the maximum value is \( [[k]] \).

stem: On the closed interval \( [0, [[b]]] \), let \( f(x) = x^{3} - 3([[p]])^{2}x \). Comparing the value of \( f \) at the endpoints and at its critical point in \( (0,[[b]]) \), what is the absolute maximum value of \( f \) on this interval?
solution: \( f'(x)=3x^{2}-3([[p]])^{2}=0 \) at \( x=[[p]] \), a local minimum since \( f \) decreases then increases. The candidates are \( f(0)=0 \), \( f([[p]])=-2([[p]])^{3} \), and \( f([[b]])=[[b]]^{3}-3([[p]])^{2}[[b]] \); since \( [[b]]>2\cdot[[p]] \) the last value is largest, so the absolute maximum is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( f(0) \), an endpoint but not the largest value.
This is the local minimum value \( f([[p]]) \), not the maximum.
This drops the \( -3([[p]])^{2}x \) term when evaluating the right endpoint.
This uses \( p \) instead of \( p^{2} \) in the endpoint evaluation.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A company's revenue from selling \( x \) units is \( R(x) = [[p0]]x - [[m]]x^{2} \) dollars, for \( x \ge 0 \). What is the maximum possible revenue, in dollars?
solution: \( R(x) \) is a downward parabola with vertex at \( x=\frac{[[p0]]}{2([[m]])} \); the maximum revenue is \( R\!\left(\frac{[[p0]]}{2\cdot[[m]]}\right)=\frac{[[p0]]^{2}}{4\cdot[[m]]}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops a factor of 2 in the denominator.
This is the optimal quantity \( x^{*} \), not the maximum revenue.
This multiplies the intercept by the coefficient instead of using the vertex formula.
This drops the factor of 4 entirely.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = x^{4} - 2([[c]])x^{2} \) for all real \( x \). What is the absolute minimum value of \( f \)?
distractor_rationales: This is the value at \( x=0 \), a local maximum, not the global minimum.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
